name: spectroflat

metadata.name: spectroflat
metadata.summary: Flat field and smile correction spectro-polarimetric solar images.

documentation:
# spectroflat
Python based library to flat field spectro-polarimetric data.

When using this library to reduce your data, please cite:

Hoelken et al. "Spectroflat: A generic spectrum and flat-field calibration library for spectro-polarimetric data" 
([DOI: 10.1051/0004-6361/202348877](https://doi.org/10.1051/0004-6361/202348877))

## Theoretical Background 
Generally this is intended to be an extension of the
"[Precise reduction of solar spectra obtained with large CCD arrays](https://www.aanda.org/articles/aa/pdf/2002/42/aa2154.pdf)"
method by Wöhl et al. (2002) to spectro-polarimetric instruments covering a large spectral field of view with many lines.

The spectroflat algorithm is described and evaluated in 
Hölken et al. (2023) "Spectroflat: A generic calibration library for spectro-polarimetric data". [in preparation] 

## Input data 
The input data must be calibrated for the camera zero-input response (dark current and bias level) and 
relevant non-linearity effects. If each modulation state is to be corrected with its own set of calibration data 
an average of all frames from the flat field recording belonging to each modulation state has to be provided. 

Input data shall be provided as a `numpy` Array with dimensions modulation state, spatial location 
along the spectrograph slit and wavelength position. 

## Extracted Data

### Dust-Flat
The dust-flat is a combination of the sensor and slit flat that 
contains most of the "hard" flat field features.
Most prominently the following is corrected:
- Sensor features (e.g. column-to-column response patterns, dust on the sensor itself)
- Slit features (e.g. dust on the slit resulting in line features in the spectral direction)
- Fixed optical fringes and illumination impurities.

The dust flat might be split in Sensor Flat and Slit Flat by the mean profile method applied along 
the spectrograph slit dimension. 

### Smile offset map
spectroflat characterizes the smile distortion by tracking the change of every spectral absorption or emission 
line with respect to the reference profile generated from the central rows. The map list the offset each pixel 
has, compared to that reference, with sub-pixel precision.  

### Illumination pattern
Typically, the illumination patterns are in the 10−6 range and are not used.  
This result is kept for compatibility with the approach of Wöhl et al. (2002).

### PDF Report
A report summary with relevant plots to inspect the quality of the extracted products. 

## Technical Documentation

**NOTE** This library expects the spacial domain on the vertical-axis and
the spectral domain on the horizontal axis. 
spectroflat does not include any file reading/writing routines and expects `numpy` arrays as input. 

Please refer to the  [API Documentation](https://hoelken.pages.gwdg.de/spectroflat/doc/spectroflat/) for 
implementation details. 
Especially the entries on available 
[Configuration](https://hoelken.pages.gwdg.de/spectroflat/doc/spectroflat/base/config.html) values 
are of general interest. 

The [`example.py`](example.py) script provides an usage example. 

## Contact
This code is developed and maintained at the Max Planck Institute for 
Solar System Research (MPS) Göttingen.

### Maintainer 
- Johannes Hoelken ([hoelken@mps.mpg.de](mailto:hoelken@mps.mpg.de))

### Contributions
- Alex Feller 
- Francisco Iglesias

## License
BSD clause-2, see [LICENSE](LICENSE)

modules.name: spectroflat.base.exceptions
documentation:
Definitions for custom exceptions go here

@author: hoelken
!/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: spectroflat.base.exceptions.IllegalStateException
documentation:
Exception to be raised if the current class is asked to
    perform an operation where the state conditions are not
    satisfied (yet). E.g. no data loaded.

classes.name: spectroflat.base.exceptions.DataMissMatchException
documentation:
Exception to be raised if the data do not fit together.
    I.e. if a dark image from another cam is used, or if the
    integration times don't match.

classes.name: spectroflat.base.exceptions.InsufficientDataException
documentation:
Exception to be raised if the data does not suffice processing.
    I.e. not enough frames to detect polarimetric state

classes.name: spectroflat.base.exceptions.IllegalArgumentException
documentation:
Exception to be raised if the current class is asked to
    perform an operation where the state conditions are not
    satisfied (yet). E.g. no data loaded.

classes.name: spectroflat.base.exceptions.MissConfigurationException
documentation:
Exception to be raised if the given configuration has internal
    conflicts or is missing mandatory information

modules.name: spectroflat.base.logging
documentation:
Logging module provides an interface to initialize
and use the python logger.

classes.name: spectroflat.base.logging.Logging

methods.name: spectroflat.base.logging.Logging.init_console
documentation: Init console logging.

methods.name: spectroflat.base.logging.Logging.init_file
documentation: Init logging to a log file.

methods.name: spectroflat.base.logging.Logging.get_logger
documentation: Retrieve the SUSI default logger

methods.name: spectroflat.base.logging.Logging.set_log_level
documentation:
Set the logging level of the application. Per default the level is set to INFO (1).

### Params
 - level: (int) The level to set, where
    - 0: DEBUG
    - 1: INFO
    - 2: WARNING
    - 3: ERROR
    - 4: CRITICAL

Facade for python logging.
Provides easy setup and configuration methods.

classes.name: spectroflat.base.sensor_flat_config.SensorFlatConfig

methods.name: spectroflat.base.sensor_flat_config.SensorFlatConfig.from_dict
documentation:
Create a sensor flat config from a dictionary.

All instance variable names are supported as keywords.
All keywords are optional, if the keyword is not present the default will be used.

### Params
- data: the dictionary to parse

### Returns
The created SensorFlatConfig

DataItem for sensor flat extraction configuration.

This class holds all the tiny little configuration details
used during the extraction of the sensor flat.

classes.name: spectroflat.base.config.Config

methods.name: spectroflat.base.config.Config.from_dict
documentation:
Create a config from a dictionary.

All instance variable names are supported as keywords.
All keywords are optional, if the keyword is not present the default will be used.
Configuration DataItem

classes.name: spectroflat.base.smile_config.SmileConfig

Create a smile config from a dictionary.

All instance variable names are supported as keywords.
All keywords are optional, if the keyword is not present the default will be used.

### Params
- data: the dictionary to parse

### Returns
The created SmileConfig

DataItem for smile detection configuration.

This class holds all the tiny little configuration details
used during smile detection.

modules.name: spectroflat.inspection.pdf
documentation: Module to provide helper methods / constants for matplotlib PDF reports
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: spectroflat.inspection.pdf.text_page
documentation:
Helper method to add a text page to a given PdfPages object

## Params
- pdf: The PdfPages object to extend
- content: The text content for the page
- ha: horizontal alignment of the text
- pos: vertical position (only used if content is a string)
- footer: Optional footer text
- page_size: Size of the page in inches.

functions.name: spectroflat.inspection.pdf.table_page
documentation:
Helper method to add a table page to a given PdfPages object

## Params
- pdf: The PdfPages object to extend
- col_labels: list of column labels (formatted strings)
- row_values: 2-D list wih row values (formatted strings)
- page_size: Size of the page in inches.

functions.name: spectroflat.inspection.offset_map.plt_map
documentation:
Generates a heatmap plot of the given smile offset map.
Note: Will only plot the average for all states (e.g. the "squashed" version, without squashing the map)

### Params
- smap: The `OffsetMap` to plot
- rows: The row numbers to plot cuts through
- title: The title of the plot

functions.name: spectroflat.inspection.flat_field_report.plt_adjustment_comparison
documentation:
Plot a cutout of selected rows from the original and the smile-corrected image.
The plot shows window//2 pixels around the strongest line in the original.

```
param original

The original image

param desmiled

The desmiled image

param pdf

The PdfPages object to save the figure to

param window

Windows size in px

param roi

region of interest, if any
```

modules.name: spectroflat.fitting.line_fit
documentation:
Module to help with fitting Gauss Curves to data

@author: hoelken
!/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: spectroflat.fitting.line_fit.gaussian
documentation:
Fitting function for [Gaussian normal distribution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Normal_distribution).

Signature follows requirements for `scipy.optimize.curve_fit` callable,
see [curve_fit documentation](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.optimize.curve_fit.html).
It takes the independent variable as the first argument and the parameters to fit as separate remaining arguments.

### Params
- `x` The free variable
- `amplitude` The amplitude
- `mean` The center of the peak
- `sigma` The standard deviation (The width of the peak)

### Returns
The y value

functions.name: spectroflat.fitting.line_fit.overlapping_gaussian
documentation:
Fitting function for data with (potentially) overlapping gaussian shaped peaks.
Parameters are similar to `gaussian`. Always only one x, but the other params may come in packs of three.

See `gaussian` for further details

functions.name: spectroflat.fitting.line_fit.lorentzian
documentation:
Fitting function for [Cauchy-Lorentzian distribution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cauchy_distribution)

Signature follows requirements for `scipy.optimize.curve_fit` callable,
see [curve_fit documentation](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.optimize.curve_fit.html).
It takes the independent variable as the first argument and the parameters to fit as separate remaining arguments.

### Params
- `x` The free variable
- `amplitude` The amplitude
- `center` The center of the peak
- `width` The width of the peak

### Returns
The y value

functions.name: spectroflat.fitting.line_fit.overlapping_lorentzian
documentation:
Fitting function for data with (potentially) overlapping lorentzian shaped peaks.
Parameters are similar to `lorentzian`. Always only one x, but the other params may come in packs of three.

See `lorentzian` for further details

classes.name: spectroflat.fitting.line_fit.LineFit

methods.name: spectroflat.fitting.line_fit.LineFit.run
documentation:
Trigger the fitting process.

### Raises
`RuntimeError` if the fit was not successful

## LineFit
Helper class to take care of fitting noise data of line profiles.

Provided a set of `x` and `y` values of same dimension the process will first look for
peaks in the `y`. Depending on the number of peaks the algorithm will try a single or
overlapping gauss fit and will compute starting amplitude, mean and sigma from the peak(s)
found.

It will first try a lorentzian fit, if this does not work it will try a gaussian fit as fallback.

The resulting optimized values, covariance and errors can be retrieved directly after the fit was performed.
Also, the x-location of the maximum (peak) is available.

modules.name: spectroflat.fitting
documentation:
## Fitting

Line detection and line fitting package provides classes to detect lines
in spectrographic images and to find the line center in a given row.

Please refer to the description of the individual modules for more details.

modules.name: spectroflat.fitting.line
documentation:
provides Line dataclass

@author: hoelken
!/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: spectroflat.fitting.line.Line

methods.name: spectroflat.fitting.line.Line.add
documentation:
Add a point to the `Line`.

```
param point

The point to add as (x,y) tuple
```

methods.name: spectroflat.fitting.line.Line.gaps
documentation: Compute distance between the `coord` coordinates of the line points

methods.name: spectroflat.fitting.line.Line.area
documentation: The lookup area for the line in a given row.
Container to store a map of line points

functions.name: spectroflat.fitting.line_detector._detect_line
documentation:
Method on module level to allow parallelization.

```
param args

Tuple with (Line, [cols to check], data)
```

classes.name: spectroflat.fitting.line_detector.LineDetector

methods.name: spectroflat.fitting.line_detector.LineDetector.run
documentation: Detect lines at anchor points

This class aims to detect absorption (or emission) lines in an image.

It first bins all rows and looks for peaks to detect the approx line center.
This reduces noise and allows for a good first estimate where to expect line(s).

Then, for each anchor row, the area around the estimated center is fitted with a gaussian
to detect the actual peak. A map of those detected peaks is available via the `lines` variable
at the end of the process.

modules.name: spectroflat.shift.sub_shift
documentation:
Util for sub pixel shifting

@author: hoelken
!/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: spectroflat.shift.sub_shift.Shifter

methods.name: spectroflat.shift.sub_shift.Shifter.d1shift
documentation:
Creates a FFTd version of the image and shifts it by the offset amount.

## Params
- `ìmg`: An 1d array object representing the image row
- `offset`: The amount the center is shifted

methods.name: spectroflat.shift.sub_shift.Shifter.d2shift
documentation:
Creates a FFTd version of the image and shifts it by the offset amount.

## Params
- `ìmg`: An array object representing the image
- `offset`: The amount the center is shifted must be float or iterable.
    If float the same offset will be applied to all axes, if iterable an offset for each axis must be provided
Helper class to shift images in subpixel amounts using FFT

classes.name: spectroflat.shift.img_rotation.RotationAnalysis

methods.name: spectroflat.shift.img_rotation.RotationAnalysis.run
documentation: Start the detection algorithm

This class analyzes the global rotation of a given image.

First it detects edges via the canny algorithm, and then it uses scikit
implementation of [Hough Transform](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hough_transform) to detect
straight lines. Finally, the angle of all lines is computed and the median of all angles is returned.

methods.name: spectroflat.shift.img_rotation.RotationCorrection.bicubic
documentation:
Rotate the given image by the given angle with a bicubic algorithm
### Params
- cut_shape: If True image will be cropped to square
- pad_mean: If True the black borders will be filled with the images mean value instead.

### Returns
The rotated image

classes.name: spectroflat.sensor.smooth_model.SmoothModel
documentation:
The `SmoothModel` is a smoothed version of the actual flat image.

It is created by fitting a polynomial of configured degree (usually 2 to 6)
to each spectral position along the spatial resolution.
As the Image typically suffers from the spectrographic curvature effect and
optical aberrations the  fitted version is an ideal representation of the
flat field frame without these.

functions.name: spectroflat.sensor.c2c_comp.extract_c2c_comp
documentation:
Removes the average column2column response comp
\:param img: the mean flat frame
\:param shape: the shape of the pattern to create
\:return: tuple (the cleaned input img, avrg. response diff)

classes.name: spectroflat.analyzer.Analyser

methods.name: spectroflat.analyzer.Analyser.run
documentation: Perform the smile analysis on the given dataset

The Analyzer is the smile detection library entry point.
This class will generate the offset map and gain table that can be used to correct science frames.

If a report dir is given, a PDF report with detailed information on the generated offset map and gain
table is created.

classes.name: spectroflat.sensor.flat.Flat

methods.name: spectroflat.sensor.flat.Flat.from_frame
documentation:
Create a `SensorFlat` from an average flat frame.
Please note that this method expects a 2d image.

The `SensorFlat` class is a pre-flat field that contains most of the hard flat field features.
Most prominently the following is corrected:
    - Sensor features (e.g. dust on the sensor itself)
    - Slit features (e.g. dust on the slit resulting in line features in the spectral direction)
    - (Some of) polarimetric fringes.

Note 1: The `SensorFlat` is not a full flat and does not correct for gradients nor all fringes.

Note 2: This class expects 2D data. It is a good idea to have such a flat per modulation state.

modules.name: spectroflat.smile.smile_correction
documentation:
provides SmileCorrector, RowCorrector

@author: hoelken
!/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: spectroflat.smile.smile_correction.SmileCorrector
documentation:
## SmileCorrector

Smile correction super class

modules.name: spectroflat.smile.interpolated_correction
documentation:
provides SmileInterpolator

@author: hoelken
!/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: spectroflat.smile.interpolated_correction.SmileInterpolator

methods.name: spectroflat.smile.interpolated_correction.SmileInterpolator.desmile_state
documentation:
Applies the smile correction to all mod states simultaneously.

### Params
data should be a tuple of:
0 = smap: OffsetMap,
1 = img: np.array, the image to correct,
2 = state: Union[int, None]

### Returns
A tuple of (state, corrected_image)

## SmileInterpolator

Uses (bi cubic) 2D interpolation to de-skew the given image according to the provided `OffsetMap`.

modules.name: spectroflat.smile
documentation: Smile detection and correction

modules.name: spectroflat.smile.smile_detection
documentation:
provides SmileMapGenerator, SmileDetector, LineSmileFitter

@author: hoelken
!/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: spectroflat.smile.smile_detection.SmileMapGenerator
documentation:
##SmileMapGenerator
This class generates a `OffsetMap` from a set of input files.
For each state: In every row lines are detected and line cores fitted.
Then the cores are shifted wrt to the central row until the chi² error
between current and central row is minimal

The `OffsetMap` can be saved to a fits file.
Metadata in the smile map with keywords similar to the FITS images will help to test
the applicability of the map for an image to correct.

classes.name: spectroflat.smile.alignment.StateAligner
documentation:
The StateAligner uses the already desmiled version of the input flat frames
to compute the differential position of all selected lines with respect to mod. state 0.
The delta will then be linear interpolated over the full range and added to the offset map of the
respective state.

modules.name: spectroflat.smile.offset_map
documentation:
provides OffsetMap

@author: hoelken
!/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: spectroflat.smile.offset_map.OffsetMap

methods.name: spectroflat.smile.offset_map.OffsetMap.dump
documentation:
Dump a SmileMap to a `fits` file.
HDU 0: shifts + header
HDU 1: chi2 error map

```
param out_file

The file to write to

param overwrite

Overwrite existing file with same name?
```

## Offset Map
This class holds the map of offsets by the smile per line.

classes.name: spectroflat.smile.smoothing.LineRemover

methods.name: spectroflat.smile.smoothing.LineRemover.run
documentation:
Iterates over all mod states in the image cube and removes the vertical features
while maintaining the vertical gradient.

class removes the vertical features (i.e. absorption/emission lines) from the flat field image.
The input datacube must be de-smiled.

classes.name: spectroflat.smile.smoothing.ResidualsRemover
documentation:
Helper to remove vertical 1px wide line residuals by interpolating over the
left and right border of the peak.

The Residual is detected by finding peaks in the horizontal average of the
central region of the image.

classes.name: spectroflat.smile.smoothing.GlobalSmudger
documentation:
## GlobalSmudger

This class is to be applied after the absorption lines have been removed from the
spectral image. As main residuals are vertically, we first generate a polynomial in
horizontal direction to get the remaining global gradients of the image.
Then, we do the same in horizontal direction. Finally, a gaussian filter is applied
to create a smooth gain table

Polynomial degree and the sigma value to ignore outliers are configurable.

modules.name: spectroflat.utils
documentation:
## Utility package

The `utils` package provides small (predominant static) utilities for recurring
tasks in the `spectroflat` code base. For example spawning threads and processes
or dealing with collections.

Please refer to the description of the individual modules for more details.

modules.name: spectroflat.utils.collections
documentation:
The `Collections` utility provides methods to deal with Collections (lists, dictionaries, arrays, ...)

@author: hoelken
!/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: spectroflat.utils.collections.Collections

methods.name: spectroflat.utils.collections.Collections.as_float_array
documentation:
Creates a copy of the orig and converts all values to `float32`

### Params
- orig: an object that can be converted to a list

### Params
Array with float values converted from the orig

methods.name: spectroflat.utils.collections.Collections.as_int_array
documentation:
Creates a copy of the orig and converts all values to `int`

### Params
- orig: an object that can be converted to a list

### Params
Array with int values converted from the orig

methods.name: spectroflat.utils.collections.Collections.bin
documentation:
Bins along a given set of axis.

### Params
- orig: The original numpy array
- binning: A list of binning values.
    - Length of the list must match the number of axis (i.e. the length of the `orig.shape`).
    - Per axis set `1` for no binning, `-1` for bin all and any positive number
          to specify the bin size along the axis.
- method: The function to apply to the bin (e.g. np.max for max pooling, np.mean for average)
### Returns
The binned array

methods.name: spectroflat.utils.collections.Collections.bin_axis
documentation:
Bins an array along a given axis.

### Params
- data: The original numpy array
- axis: The axis to bin along
- binsize: The size of each bin
- method: The function to apply to the bin (e.g. np.max for max pooling, np.mean for average)

### Returns
The binned array

methods.name: spectroflat.utils.collections.Collections.replace_sigma_outliers
documentation:
Replaces outliers from the data set with the mean.

```
param data

The data to clean

param s

the factor of sigma to clean for. Default is 5 Sigma (99.99994%)

return

A cleaned copy of the dataset
```

methods.name: spectroflat.utils.collections.Collections.interpolate_sigma_outliers
documentation: Interpolate pixels/areas in 1D(!) arrays that are below or above a given sigma range
Static utility for handling collections.

modules.name: spectroflat.utils.processing
documentation:
Utilities for (multi) processing

@author: hoelken
!/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: spectroflat.utils.operations.save_divide
documentation:
Method to safely divide one array by the other.
Avoids division by zero and improves numerical precision.

functions.name: spectroflat.utils.ffing.split_sensor_slit_flat
documentation:
Splits the given dust_flat into the sensor and slit contribution parts.
\:param dust_flat: the dust flat to split
\:param rotation: The rotation of the dust flat (see offset map header)
\:return: tuple(sensor, slit)

functions.name: spectroflat.utils.polynomial.min_value
documentation:
Return the minimum value of the polynomial within the given range
(or globally, if no range is given)

functions.name: spectroflat.utils.polynomial.min_pos
documentation:
Return the position of the minimum of the polynomial within the given range
(or globally, if no range is given)

functions.name: spectroflat.utils.polynomial.max_value
documentation:
Return the maximum value of the polynomial within the given range
(or globally, if no range is given)

functions.name: spectroflat.utils.polynomial.max_pos
documentation:
Return the position of the maximum of the polynomial within the given range
(or globally, if no range is given)

functions.name: spectroflat.utils.polynomial.abs_max_value
documentation:
Return the maximum of the absolute extreme values within the given range
(or globally, if no range is given)
